taxonomy.sub_category: Aqua-life Desalination module Updated on February 2, 2024 · Created on October 22, 2021 Aqua-life Desalination module Water desalination using wave energy. Developed By Aqua-life Tested By TUV SUD Management Service GmbH Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description Aqua-life is a system that integrates seawater into the water desalination process. They manufacture various products ranging from high-pressure pumps to desalination systems consisting of modules and plants that can suit any market requirements accordingly. The different models are capable of handling water from 25 m 3 /d up to 100 m 3 /d. This product targets Private & public sectors, businesses and others with desalination needs. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household, Small and Medium-sized Enterprises, Public Sector Agencies Distributors / Implementing Organizations Distributed throughout the globe through a selected number of stores as shown in Distributors and purchasing Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include BlueBox RO DS , Aero-Desalinator , Desolenator , and Eliodomestico Solar Water Distiller . Regions Worldwide Manufacturing/Building Method The technology was developed in Russia. The product is now manufactured in Poland. Intellectural Property Type Patent User Provision Model The users can purchase the product from 40 stores across the globe. Distributors and Purchasing have a list of stores and distributors from which the products can be purchased. Distributions to Date Status As of 2021, 4 Aqua-life desalination modules have been installed in India, Malaysia, Italy, and Tanzania. Type of system Sea water desalination module Manufacturer-specified water treatment rate (L/hr) 1041.67 L/hr Bacteria reduction 1 µm Virus reduction 0.01 µm Protozoa reduction 10 µm Heavy metals and/or arsenic reduction 0.01 µm Power required Wave energy Safe water storage capacity (L) Unknown Design Specifications Aqua-life builds different types of seawater desalination systems. The system includes three different blocks: the pre-treatment block, RO block, and desalinated water storage block. It uses a high-pressure pump combined with an energy recovery exchanger to reduce energy consumption and increase efficiency. The system is manufactured for two conditions: either as an indoor unit or as an outdoor unit. The indoor unit is a frame-mounted unit and the outdoor unit is a containerized type plant. Product Schematics Technical Support Provided by the manufacturer Replacement Components Unknown Lifecycle Unknown Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters Reduced energy consumption through the use of external energy recovery device. Vetted Performance Status 60% of the driven energy of the system comes from the high-pressure brine that passes through the unique pump. This is due to the integrated energy recovery device. Safety Unknown Complementary Technical Systems The manufacturer also sells a patented high pressure pump that has an integrated built-in internal energy recovery device; individual reverse osmosis plants integrated with the energy recovery system; a range of desalination systems for seawater vessels. Academic Research and References “Aqualife – Desalination technology. Sea water RO. Contacts,” Aqua Life desalination technologies. https://aqua-life.pl/contacts/ “Competitive landscape,” Engineering For Change. https://www.engineeringforchange.org/solutions/products/?category=desalination-evaporation “Goal 6,” Sdgs.un.org. https://sdgs.un.org/goals/goal6 “About Aqua Life desalination technologies High pressure pump for sea RO,” Aqua Life desalination technologies, 19-Mar-2021. https://aqua-life.pl/about-aqua-life-desalination/ “Desalination plants,” Aqua Life desalination technologies, 25-Mar-2021. https://aqua-life.pl/products/desalination-plants/ “Desalination plants,” Aqua Life desalination technologies, 25-Mar-2021. https://aqua-life.pl/products/desalination-plants/#images-4 “APPR Pumps Pumps for seawater RO – Aqua_life desalination systems,” Aqua Life desalination technologies, 29-Mar-2021. Available: http://aqua-life.pl/products/appr-pumps/ Compliance with regulations The system is designed in compliance with the ISO 9001:2015 standard as mentioned in the Quality Certification Reference. Evaluation methods Based on low energy consumption, high efficiency, compact size, and reliability as stated on the Product site. Other Information None Comments from the Community 1 Comment bingyaoli says: January 24, 2025 at 11:58 pm Utilizing wave energy is a great innovation that reduces energy costs and can play an important role in areas that are near the sea and lack fresh water. However, the product description does not mention pricing information. Retail price and subsequent maintenance costs are very important factors for users when assessing the feasibility of the equipment, and it would be good to add relevant data.
